title: Marketing-sourced deals close 13 days faster this week
anchor: Current week wins average 38 days to close versus 51 days over the prior 11 weeks.
connect: Seven of nine wins came from mid-market accounts.
body: Current week wins average 38 days to close versus 51 days over the prior 11 weeks. Seven of nine wins came from mid-market accounts.
trace: Marketing-sourced win velocity compressed significantly in the current week compared to prior 11-week baseline.

title: Enterprise win rate jumps to 31% in Q2
anchor: Q2 enterprise deals close at 30.77% versus 22.22% over the trailing four quarters.
connect: Average deal size increased to $187K from the four-quarter average of $142K.
body: Q2 enterprise deals close at 30.77% versus 22.22% over the trailing four quarters. Average deal size increased to $187K from the four-quarter average of $142K.
trace: Enterprise segment showing both higher win rate and higher ASP in Q2 compared to trailing four quarter baseline.

title: Event-sourced deals move 25 days faster this quarter
anchor: Q1 event wins close at 45.3 days versus Q4's 70.6 days.
connect: SaaS Connect delivered all 20 event-sourced wins in Q1.
body: Q1 event wins close at 45.3 days versus Q4's 70.6 days. SaaS Connect delivered all 20 event-sourced wins in Q1.
trace: Event pipeline velocity improved significantly Q1 vs Q4, with SaaS Connect as the sole event source for closed wins.

title: Mid-market SQL volume up 50% in April
anchor: April mid-market SQLs hit 147 versus the Q1 monthly average of 98.
connect: ABM acceptance rate runs at 61.4% compared to 41.6% for non-ABM leads.
body: April mid-market SQLs hit 147 versus the Q1 monthly average of 98. ABM acceptance rate runs at 61.4% compared to 41.6% for non-ABM leads.
trace: Mid-market SQL generation accelerated significantly in April, with ABM leads showing higher SDR acceptance.

title: Paid social overtakes paid search for pipeline creation
anchor: Q2 paid social generates $620K in pipeline versus $380K from paid search.
connect: Q1 showed the reverse pattern at $720K paid search and $410K paid social.
body: Q2 paid social generates $620K in pipeline versus $380K from paid search. Q1 showed the reverse pattern at $720K paid search and $410K paid social.
trace: Channel mix shifted Q1 to Q2 with paid social becoming the lead pipeline generator over paid search.

title: Branded search climbs 92% over six weeks
anchor: Weekly branded search volume moved from 1000 to 1921 with increases each week.
connect: Six consecutive weeks of growth through April 19.
body: Weekly branded search volume moved from 1000 to 1921 with increases each week. Six consecutive weeks of growth through April 19.
trace: Branded search showing consistent week-over-week growth pattern with significant cumulative increase.

title: Atlas brand mentions lead competitor by 52%
anchor: April Atlas mentions hit 1840 versus Beacon Systems at 1210.
connect: Analyst mentions increased to 9 in the last 14 days from 7 in the prior 30.
body: April Atlas mentions hit 1840 versus Beacon Systems at 1210. Analyst mentions increased to 9 in the last 14 days from 7 in the prior 30.
trace: Brand mention volume ahead of top competitor with analyst coverage accelerating in recent two weeks.

title: Direct traffic crosses organic for the first time
anchor: April traffic runs 42.5% direct versus 36.4% organic search.
connect: Direct first exceeded organic on April 15.
body: April traffic runs 42.5% direct versus 36.4% organic search. Direct first exceeded organic on April 15.
trace: Direct traffic share exceeded organic search for the first time on April 15, maintaining lead through month end.

title: Atlas Insights reference opt-in hits 76%
anchor: Reference participation sits at 75.7% for Atlas Insights and 70.7% for Atlas Workflow.
connect: Atlas Connect lags at 28.2% opt-in rate.
body: Reference participation sits at 75.7% for Atlas Insights and 70.7% for Atlas Workflow. Atlas Connect lags at 28.2% opt-in rate.
trace: Reference pool strength varies significantly by product with Insights and Workflow showing high advocacy versus Connect.

title: Three target accounts hit high-intent threshold
anchor: Current week shows 3 accounts with 5+ high-intent events from a universe of 50 targets.
connect: March ABM adds contributed 1 account and Named Accounts list contributed 2.
body: Current week shows 3 accounts with 5+ high-intent events from a universe of 50 targets. March ABM adds contributed 1 account and Named Accounts list contributed 2.
trace: High-intent account activity concentrated in 3 of 50 target accounts this week, split across target list segments.

title: SDR team runs 68 leads under capacity
anchor: Current week capacity sits at 210 with inbound volume at 142.
connect: Trailing four weeks averaged 169 inbound versus 212 capacity.
body: Current week capacity sits at 210 with inbound volume at 142. Trailing four weeks averaged 169 inbound versus 212 capacity.
trace: SDR team has excess capacity both current week and on trailing four-week average basis.

title: Mid-market fintech accounts drive recent wins
anchor: Last 60 days show 12 mid-market closed-wins with 10 from fintech industry.
connect: All 12 accounts run Snowflake plus dbt in their stack.
body: Last 60 days show 12 mid-market closed-wins with 10 from fintech industry. All 12 accounts run Snowflake plus dbt in their stack.
trace: Mid-market win concentration in fintech vertical with consistent technology stack pattern across all recent wins.

title: Digital ads budget reallocated to events
anchor: Q2 digital spend tracks at $256K versus $340K planned with $84K unspent.
connect: Reallocation targets SaaS Connect and Signal Summit.
body: Q2 digital spend tracks at $256K versus $340K planned with $84K unspent. Reallocation targets SaaS Connect and Signal Summit.
trace: Q2 digital ads underspend being reallocated to two specific event properties rather than remaining in digital channels.

title: Atlas Assist launch locks calendar this week
anchor: Product launch scheduled for May 8 with campaign ready status.
body: Product launch scheduled for May 8 with campaign ready status. Calendar locks April 24.
trace: Atlas Assist product launch on track with campaign prepared and calendar coordination completing this week.

title: Forrester and G2 drive analyst mention volume
anchor: Last 14 days analyst coverage shows 9 mentions with Forrester and G2 each contributing 3.
connect: Gartner added 1 mention in the same period.
body: Last 14 days analyst coverage shows 9 mentions with Forrester and G2 each contributing 3. Gartner added 1 mention in the same period.
trace: Analyst mention acceleration driven primarily by Forrester and G2 coverage rather than distributed across all major firms.
